---
format: html
title: classify_to_functional_type()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>classify_to_functional_type</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Classify Community Data to Functional Types</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Classifies taxa in a long-format community data frame to
functional-type (FT) labels using a pre-computed FT
classification table. Aggregates pollen proportions by all
non-'taxon'/'value' identifier columns plus functional type.
Produces the same
output column structure as 'classify_taxonomic_resolution()'
so it can be used as a drop-in replacement downstream.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>classify_to_functional_type(data, data_ft_classification, verbose = TRUE)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data">data</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame containing community data with columns
'taxon', 'dataset_name', 'age', and 'value'. Other identifier
columns such as 'sample_name' are preserved.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_ft_classification">data_ft_classification</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame mapping taxa to functional types. Must contain
columns 'taxon_name' (character) and 'functional_type'
(integer). Typically produced by 'cluster_functional_types()'
and loaded via 'get_functional_type_classification()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="verbose">verbose</code></td>
<td>
<p>Logical. If 'TRUE' (default), informational progress messages
are printed to the console via 'cli'. Note: the warning issued
when taxa are absent from 'data_ft_classification' is always
emitted regardless of this flag.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Steps performed:
</p>

<ol>
<li><p> Validate arguments.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Left-join 'data' to 'data_ft_classification' on
'data$taxon == data_ft_classification$taxon_name'.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Drop unmatched taxa (NA functional type) with a
warning.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Create 'taxon' labels '&quot;FT_functional_type&quot;'.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Aggregate 'value' by original identifier columns and
'taxon'.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> Full-join back to an identifier/taxon cross-reference
to preserve true negatives.
</p>
</li></ol>



<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A data frame with the same column names as 'data'. The
'taxon' column is replaced by functional-type labels of the
form '&quot;FT_1&quot;', '&quot;FT_2&quot;', etc. 'value' is aggregated
(summed) by all original identifier columns and 'taxon'. All
identifier combinations present after FT classification are
preserved (true negatives kept via a cross-reference join).
Taxa not found in 'data_ft_classification' are dropped with
a 'cli::cli_warn()' message.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[classify_taxonomic_resolution()],
[get_functional_type_classification()],
[cluster_functional_types()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
